description: Migrate LLM-as-judge into CCB, add dual-scoring (verifier + judge), statistical rigor (bootstrap CIs), verifier hardening (debug mode, self-tests), and oracle auto-discovery. Single reproducible evaluation package.

userStories.title: Judge data models and prompt templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational data models and prompt templates for the judge module so that the engine, oracle, and CLI can build on them.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/ directory exists with __init__.py (empty for now)

scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/models.py defines: JudgeInput (task_id, task_description, code_changes, tool_calls, verifier_reward, oracle_ground_truth, oracle_expected_approach, oracle_evaluation_criteria:list[str], oracle_context_files:list[str], mcp_tools_used:list[str]), JudgeResult (task_id, benchmark, config, judge_score:float, dimension_scores:dict, oracle_confidence:str, model:str, temperature:float, rounds:int, vote_distribution:dict, judged_at:str, provenance:dict), OracleBundle (ground_truth_text, expected_approach, evaluation_criteria:list[str], context_files:list[str], confidence:str)
All dataclasses use @dataclass with type hints; stdlib only (dataclasses, typing)
JudgeResult has to_dict() method that produces JSON-serializable output matching schemas/judge_result.schema.json
normalize_score() function maps strings (pass/partial/fail/1.0/0.5/0.0) to float values
scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/prompts.py defines three prompt template constants: REFERENCE_CORRECTNESS_PROMPT, REFERENCE_COMPLETENESS_PROMPT, DIRECT_REVIEW_PROMPT as Python triple-quoted strings
Each prompt template has {placeholders} for: task_description, agent_output, reference_answer, evaluation_criteria, context_files
Each prompt specifies JSON output format with: reasoning, dimension_scores (correctness/completeness/code_quality/retrieval_quality/efficiency each with score+evidence), overall_score, confidence
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/models.py succeeds
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/prompts.py succeeds
userStories.notes: Foundation for all other judge stories. No external deps. Port structure from Dashboard's src/benchmark/llm_judge.py (EnhancedJudgeInput lines 30-55, EnhancedJudgeAssessment lines 60-95). Use 3-point scale: 1.0=pass, 0.5=partial, 0.0=fail.

userStories.title: Anthropic API backend with retry logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a thin wrapper around the Anthropic API that handles authentication, retries, and response parsing so the judge engine doesn't contain HTTP plumbing.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/backends.py exists with AnthropicBackend class
AnthropicBackend.__init__ accepts model:str, temperature:float=0.0, max_tokens:int=4096
AnthropicBackend.call(system_prompt:str, user_prompt:str) -> dict parses JSON from response content
Uses anthropic SDK if importable, falls back to urllib.request with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
Exponential backoff on rate limits: retry up to 3 times with 2^n second delays
Raises JudgeBackendError(message, status_code) on non-retryable failures
Response parsing extracts JSON from markdown code blocks if model wraps output in ```json```
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/backends.py succeeds
userStories.notes: Keep thin — ~100 lines. The anthropic SDK is available in Harbor Docker images. For host-side use, users run pip install anthropic. urllib fallback covers environments where SDK is missing.

userStories.title: Inter-judge agreement metrics
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need Cohen's kappa and Fleiss' kappa implementations so that ensemble judge results include agreement statistics.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/agreement.py exists
cohens_kappa(ratings_a:list[float], ratings_b:list[float]) -> float computes Cohen's kappa for two raters
fleiss_kappa(ratings_matrix:list[list[int]]) -> float computes Fleiss' kappa for 3+ raters
Both handle edge cases: all-same ratings returns 1.0, empty input returns 0.0

agreement_report(round_scores:list[list[float]]) -> dict returns {kappa, interpretation, n_rounds, n_tasks} where interpretation is 'poor'/'fair'/'moderate'/'substantial'/'almost_perfect' per Landis-Koch scale
Stdlib only (no scipy, no numpy)
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/agreement.py succeeds
Inline doctests pass: python3 -m doctest scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/agreement.py
userStories.notes: Port from Dashboard's src/judge/metrics.py (cohens_kappa, fleiss_kappa). Simplify to stdlib-only. Add Landis-Koch interpretation thresholds: <0.0 poor, 0-0.20 slight, 0.21-0.40 fair, 0.41-0.60 moderate, 0.61-0.80 substantial, 0.81-1.00 almost_perfect.

userStories.title: Core LLM judge engine with multi-round voting
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I need the core judge engine that constructs prompts, calls the API, parses responses, and optionally does multi-round voting so that tasks get scored.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/engine.py exists with LLMJudge class
LLMJudge.__init__ accepts model:str, temperature:float=0.0, rounds:int=1, dimensions:list[str]=None (defaults to all 5)

LLMJudge.evaluate(judge_input:JudgeInput) -> JudgeResult runs single-round evaluation: selects prompt template based on oracle presence, renders with input data, calls backend, parses dimension scores

LLMJudge.evaluate_with_voting(judge_input:JudgeInput, rounds:int=3) -> JudgeResult runs N rounds, majority-votes each dimension score, computes confidence=majority_count/rounds, builds vote_distribution
judge_score is weighted average of dimensions: correctness=0.30, completeness=0.25, code_quality=0.20, retrieval_quality=0.15, efficiency=0.10
Selects REFERENCE_CORRECTNESS_PROMPT when oracle has ground_truth_text, DIRECT_REVIEW_PROMPT otherwise
Provenance dict includes: model_id, temperature, rounds, ccb_commit (from git rev-parse HEAD), judge_version='1.0.0'
python3 -c "from ccb_metrics.judge.engine import LLMJudge" succeeds (run from scripts/ directory)
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/engine.py succeeds
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (models, prompts), US-002 (backend), US-003 (agreement). Core logic ported from Dashboard's EnhancedLLMJudge._evaluate_single and _evaluate_with_voting (lines 400-502). Keep under 250 lines.

userStories.title: Judge package init and unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the package __init__.py to export public API and unit tests with mocked API responses to validate the engine without real API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/__init__.py exports: LLMJudge, JudgeInput, JudgeResult, OracleBundle, normalize_score
tests/test_judge_engine.py exists with at least 6 test cases using unittest.mock.patch to mock AnthropicBackend.call
Test: single-round evaluate with oracle produces JudgeResult with all 5 dimension scores
Test: single-round evaluate without oracle uses DIRECT_REVIEW_PROMPT
Test: evaluate_with_voting with 3 rounds produces vote_distribution and confidence
Test: normalize_score maps 'pass'->1.0, 'partial'->0.5, 'fail'->0.0, '1.0'->1.0, 'garbage'->0.0
Test: JudgeResult.to_dict() produces valid JSON matching judge_result schema fields
Test: backend retry on rate limit (mock 429 then success)
python3 -m pytest tests/test_judge_engine.py passes (or python3 -m unittest tests.test_judge_engine if no pytest)
userStories.notes: Tests should be runnable without API key (all API calls mocked). Mock responses should return realistic JSON matching the prompt output format.

userStories.title: Oracle data auto-discovery from ground truth
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I want the judge to auto-load ground truth and evaluation criteria from CCB's existing data so I never manually construct oracle inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/oracle.py exists with discover_oracle(task_id:str, benchmark:str, benchmarks_dir:Path) -> OracleBundle

Discovery priority chain: (1) tests/ground_truth.json -> (2) tests/expected_defects.json -> (3) tests/expected_changes.json -> (4) solution/solve.sh patch extraction -> (5) instruction.md keyword extraction -> (6) configs/ground_truth_files.json fallback
For NL tasks (ccb_document, ccb_understand, ccb_design): extracts scoring categories from ground_truth.json as evaluation_criteria list
For code tasks (ccb_fix, ccb_build): extracts expected file paths as context_files, patch/diff content as ground_truth_text
For code review tasks (ccb_test with expected_defects.json): extracts defect descriptions as evaluation_criteria
Returns confidence='high' for structured GT (ground_truth.json, expected_defects.json), 'medium' for instruction extraction, 'low' for regex/fallback
Handles missing data gracefully: returns OracleBundle with empty fields and confidence='low' rather than raising
Task lookup handles both ccb_* directory structure (benchmarks/ccb_build/task-name/) and legacy flat layout
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/judge/oracle.py succeeds
userStories.notes: Reuse extraction logic from scripts/ccb_metrics/ground_truth.py (_gt_sdlc, _gt_swebenchpro, _gt_pytorch patterns). The ground_truth.py module already handles all the file-format parsing — oracle.py wraps it to produce OracleBundle objects. BENCHMARKS_DIR is typically /home/stephanie_jarmak/CodeContextBench/benchmarks.

userStories.title: Post-verifier judge runner CLI
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I want to run python3 scripts/run_judge.py --run <run_dir> and get judge_result.json for every task in the run.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/run_judge.py exists with argparse CLI: --run DIR (required), --suite FILTER, --task FILTER, --model MODEL_ID (default claude-haiku-4-5-20251001), --rounds N (default 1), --temperature FLOAT (default 0.0), --ensemble (enables 3-round voting), --dry-run, --force
Discovers task dirs using ccb_metrics.discovery module (discover_runs or manual scan of config/batch/task__hash/ pattern)

For each task: loads result.json for verifier_reward and task_id, loads claude-code.txt transcript via ccb_metrics.extractors, calls discover_oracle(), constructs JudgeInput, calls LLMJudge.evaluate()
Writes judge_result.json alongside result.json in each task directory
Skips tasks that already have judge_result.json unless --force is passed
--dry-run prints task list with oracle confidence without calling API
Progress output: [N/TOTAL] task_id ... correctness=X completeness=Y judge_score=Z (confidence=C)
Uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor with max_workers=4 for parallelism
Exit code 0 if all succeed, 1 if any failures with summary of failed task_ids
python3 scripts/run_judge.py --help prints usage without errors
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (engine) and US-006 (oracle). Pattern follows existing scripts like aggregate_status.py for run directory scanning. Use SKIP_PATTERNS from ir_analysis.py to exclude archive/broken dirs. Transcript extraction uses ccb_metrics.extractors.extract_tool_usage_from_transcript.

userStories.title: Bootstrap confidence intervals and paired delta module
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need bootstrap CIs on aggregate scores and paired bootstrap tests for config deltas so I can assess statistical significance.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/statistics.py exists (new file)
bootstrap_ci(values:list[float], n_bootstrap:int=1000, ci:float=0.95) -> tuple[float,float,float] returns (mean, ci_lower, ci_upper)

paired_bootstrap_delta(values_a:list[float], values_b:list[float], n_bootstrap:int=1000, ci:float=0.95) -> tuple[float,float,float,float] returns (mean_delta, ci_lower, ci_upper, p_value) where p_value is proportion of bootstrap deltas crossing 0
spearman_rank_correlation(x:list[float], y:list[float]) -> tuple[float,float] returns (rho, p_value) using rank-based formula

retrieval_outcome_correlation(ir_scores:list[float], rewards:list[float], suite_labels:list[str]) -> dict with overall and per-suite Spearman rho, p_value, effect_size (mean reward for recall>=0.8 vs <0.5)
Uses only stdlib: random.choices for bootstrap, no numpy/scipy
random.seed(42) used inside bootstrap functions for reproducibility
Inline doctests: python3 -m doctest scripts/ccb_metrics/statistics.py passes
python3 -m py_compile scripts/ccb_metrics/statistics.py succeeds
userStories.notes: Independent of judge stories. Spearman uses stdlib: rank values, compute Pearson on ranks. P-value for Spearman approximated via t-distribution or bootstrap. Keep module under 200 lines.

userStories.title: Dual-score integration in generate_manifest.py
userStories.description: As a report consumer, I want MANIFEST.json entries to include judge_score when available so downstream tools can access both scores.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/generate_manifest.py reads judge_result.json (if present) alongside result.json for each task

MANIFEST entries include optional fields: judge_score (float), judge_model (str), judge_dimensions (dict of dimension:score), judge_confidence (float) — all null/absent when no judge_result.json exists
Backwards compatible: runs without judge_result.json produce identical MANIFEST output as before this change
python3 scripts/generate_manifest.py runs without errors on existing runs/official/ data
grep 'judge_score' on MANIFEST output shows null for entries without judge data (not missing key)
userStories.notes: Read generate_manifest.py first to understand the merge logic. Judge fields should be added in the per-task dict builder, not in the merge/dedup logic. Use .get() with None defaults.

userStories.title: Dual-score tables in generate_eval_report.py
userStories.description: As a report consumer, I want the eval report to show both verifier and judge scores with divergence flagging and correlation analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/generate_eval_report.py includes judge_score column in per-task tables when judge data present in MANIFEST
Per-task table columns: task_id | verifier_reward | judge_score | delta | oracle_confidence
Tasks where abs(verifier_reward - judge_score) > 0.3 are flagged with [DIVERGENT] marker
Aggregate tables show mean_verifier and mean_judge per suite and config
Report includes Pearson r correlation between verifier_reward and judge_score per suite (using statistics.correlation from stdlib if Python 3.10+, else manual calculation)
When no judge data present, report output is identical to current behavior
python3 scripts/generate_eval_report.py runs without errors
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (manifest has judge data). Read generate_eval_report.py first. Add judge columns conditionally — check if any task in the manifest has judge_score != null before adding column headers.

userStories.title: GT confidence gating and retrieval correlation in IR analysis
userStories.description: As an analyst, I want IR aggregates to exclude low-confidence ground truth and to see retrieval-outcome correlation stratified by suite.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ir_analysis.py accepts --min-confidence flag with choices: high, medium, low (default: medium)
When --min-confidence medium, tasks with confidence='low' excluded from aggregate metrics but shown in per-task output with [low-conf] marker
--min-confidence low includes everything (backwards compatible with current behavior)
Aggregate tables include n (task count) column
Per-suite confidence breakdown printed: e.g., ccb_build: 5 high, 2 medium, 1 low
scripts/ir_analysis.py accepts --correlate flag that triggers retrieval_outcome_correlation from statistics.py
--correlate outputs per-suite Spearman rho, p-value, effect size, and scatter data as JSON (task_id, file_recall, reward)
python3 scripts/ir_analysis.py --help shows new flags
python3 scripts/ir_analysis.py runs without errors (existing behavior preserved when no new flags used)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 (statistics module). Confidence data comes from ground_truth_files.json which already has a confidence field per task. Load it alongside the GT registry.

userStories.title: Verifier debug mode
userStories.description: As a developer debugging a verifier failure, I want to set DEBUG_MODE=true and get full diagnostics captured automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/lib/verifier_lib.sh (or wherever verifier_lib.sh lives) checks DEBUG_MODE env var at top of file
When DEBUG_MODE=true: enables set -x, creates /logs/verifier/debug/ directory

Captures to /logs/verifier/debug/: environment.txt (filtered env, excluding secrets matching *KEY* *TOKEN* *SECRET*), workspace_git_status.txt, workspace_git_diff.txt (head -500 to cap size), workspace_file_tree.txt (find /workspace -type f | head -200)
Debug capture runs BEFORE the main verification logic so it captures pre-verification state
Harbor result.json is NOT affected — debug files only in /logs/verifier/debug/
configs/_common.sh passes DEBUG_MODE to Docker via -e DEBUG_MODE when the env var is set on host
docs/ERROR_CATALOG.md has new section: '## Verifier Debug Mode' explaining usage: DEBUG_MODE=true ./configs/test_2config.sh
Debug capture adds < 2 seconds to verifier execution
userStories.notes: Independent of judge stories. Find verifier_lib.sh location with: grep -r 'verifier_lib' benchmarks/ — it may be in benchmarks/lib/ or copied into each task's tests/. The env filter should use grep -vE 'KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASSWORD' to avoid leaking credentials.

userStories.title: Enhanced defect annotation data model for code review
userStories.description: As a task author, I want to annotate defects with type and line range in expected_defects.json so future scoring can match defects precisely.
acceptanceCriteria: Document new optional fields in docs/SCORING_SEMANTICS.md under new section 'Defect Annotation Format': defect_type (enum: null-deref, resource-leak, race-condition, injection, logic-error, buffer-overflow, use-after-free, other), line_start (int), line_end (int)
All 8 code review tasks in benchmarks/ccb_test/ that have expected_defects.json are updated with defect_type for every defect entry
line_start and line_end added where the defect location is unambiguous (at least 4 of 8 tasks)
scripts/ccb_metrics/ground_truth.py _gt_sdlc() extracts defect_type when present and includes it in the ground truth registry output
Existing scoring logic is NOT changed — this is data-model-only
python3 scripts/ccb_metrics/ground_truth.py runs without errors and newly extracted defect_type fields appear in output
userStories.notes: Find code review tasks: grep -rl 'expected_defects' benchmarks/ccb_test/. Defect types should be inferred from the defect descriptions in each file. This is a data enrichment story, not a scoring change.

userStories.title: Verifier fixture directories for self-testing
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I want fixture directories with known-score inputs so that verifier self-tests can validate scoring correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 tasks (one per verifier type) have tests/fixtures/ directory with: perfect_input/ (score >= 0.9), empty_input/ (score == 0.0)

Verifier types covered: (1) IR_checklist — pick one ccb_build task, (2) checklist/pattern — pick one ccb_test task, (3) diff_similarity — pick one ccb_fix pytorch task, (4) find_and_prove — pick one ccb_debug task, (5) similarity — pick one ccb_document task
perfect_input/ contains files the verifier expects for a perfect score (e.g., solution.md with all keywords for checklist, or matching diff for diff_similarity)
empty_input/ contains empty/minimal files that should score 0.0
Each fixture directory has a metadata.json: {expected_min_score: float, expected_max_score: float, verifier_type: str, description: str}
Fixture files are realistic but minimal — just enough to trigger scoring, not full agent outputs
A README.md in docs/ or inline in the fixture dirs explains the format for future task authors
userStories.notes: Requires reading each verifier type's test.sh to understand what inputs it expects. For IR_checklist: needs solution.md with file paths. For checklist/pattern: needs code changes matching keyword patterns. For diff_similarity: needs a git diff matching expected.diff. For find_and_prove: needs a test file. For similarity: needs analysis output matching keywords.

userStories.title: Verifier self-tests and idempotency in preflight
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I want validate_tasks_preflight.py to run verifiers against fixtures and check idempotency, catching bugs before runs.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/validate_tasks_preflight.py --smoke-runtime detects tests/fixtures/ directories and runs verifier against each fixture
For each fixture: copies fixture files into a temp workspace, runs test.sh, reads /logs/verifier/reward.txt, compares against metadata.json expected_min_score/expected_max_score
Reports: PASS: task-name verifier_type perfect=0.95 empty=0.0 or FAIL: task-name perfect=0.30 (expected >= 0.9)
Idempotency check (--idempotency flag or included in --smoke-runtime): runs verifier twice on same perfect_input, asserts scores match within epsilon=0.001
Non-idempotent verifiers logged as WARNING (not blocking) with both scores shown
Summary line: Verifier self-tests: N/M pass, K non-idempotent
Failures on fixture scoring exit with code 1 and clear error listing which tasks failed
python3 scripts/validate_tasks_preflight.py --help shows new flags
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014 (fixtures exist). The smoke-runtime flag already exists in validate_tasks_preflight.py — extend its logic to also check fixtures when present. Running test.sh outside Docker requires simulating the container environment (set WORKSPACE, create /logs/verifier/), which may need a lightweight wrapper.
